Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 5 is the company's latest mid-size flagship, sitting between the smaller Haiku and the massive Opus models. It's designed to deliver near-Opus quality at significantly lower cost and latency, making it the ideal workhorse for production applications.
Both GPT-5.5 Pro and Claude Sonnet 5 are exceptional models, but they have different strengths:
Both models are available through GlbTOKEN at competitive rates. You pay from your unified token balance — no separate subscriptions needed:

Claude Sonnet 5 excels at code generation, scoring 90.3% on HumanEval and 87.5% on SWE-bench (real-world software engineering tasks). It's particularly strong at code review, bug detection, and refactoring.
Claude Sonnet 5 produces significantly better long-form content than its predecessor, with improved coherence over 4,000+ word outputs. It's excellent for reports, articles, and creative writing.

Claude Sonnet 5 runs on Anthropic's latest constitutional AI framework, which provides more nuanced ethical reasoning while maintaining higher refusal accuracy. The model is 35% less likely to refuse legitimate requests compared to Claude Sonnet 4, while maintaining strong safety guardrails.
Claude Sonnet 5 supports up to 200K tokens of context with near-perfect recall across the entire window. In needle-in-a-haystack tests, it achieves 99.7% recall accuracy.
Double the context length of GPT-5, allowing you to process entire codebases, lengthy legal documents, or extended conversation histories in a single pass.
GPT-5.5 Pro can now call up to 64 functions in parallel with improved parameter extraction, making it ideal for complex agentic workflows.

GPT-5.5 Pro is the latest iteration in OpenAI's GPT series, building on the foundation of GPT-5 with substantial improvements across reasoning, factual accuracy, and tool use. It was quietly released in late June 2026 and has quickly become one of the highest-rated models on independent benchmarks.

GPT-5.5 introduces a two-pass reasoning system that internally verifies its chain-of-thought before generating output. This has reduced logical errors by approximately 40% compared to GPT-5 on complex multi-step problems.

On standard benchmarks, GPT-5.5 Pro scores 92.4% on MMLU-Pro, 89.1% on HumanEval (Python code generation), and achieves a 96.2% accuracy on GSM-8K (grade-school math). These numbers place it at the top of the leaderboard alongside Claude Sonnet 5.
Significant gains in non-English languages, particularly Mandarin, Arabic, and Swahili. Benchmarks show a 22% improvement in translation accuracy for low-resource language pairs.
Thanks to a new sparse attention mechanism, GPT-5.5 Pro delivers responses 1.8x faster than GPT-5 while maintaining the same quality level.
The model processes images, PDFs, charts, and diagrams with high accuracy. On DocVQA, it scores 93.7%, making it ideal for document-heavy workflows like invoice processing and contract analysis.

Paystack is PCI-DSS Level 1 compliant — the highest standard of payment security — and all transactions are encrypted end-to-end. We never store your card or bank details on our servers. Paystack handles all sensitive payment data directly through their secure, tokenized infrastructure.

Paystack is one of the most trusted payment processors on the African continent, serving over 70,000 businesses across multiple countries. Acquired by Stripe in 2020, Paystack provides enterprise-grade payment infrastructure with deep local integration — supporting each country's preferred payment methods, regulatory frameworks, and banking rails.

blog-article-5.html: . All GlbTOKEN traffic — API calls, dashboard sessions, webhook deliveries, and internal service-to-service communication — is encrypted using TLS 1.3 with strong cipher suites (TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384). We enforce HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) with a two-year max-age directive. Our certificate management is fully automated using ACME protocol with 90-day certificate rotation.

. An accredited third-party auditor spent months examining our controls, policies, and operations. They verified that our security practices meet the AICPA's strict criteria — not once, but over a sustained period. This isn't a self-assessment or a checkbox exercise. It's the gold standard for SaaS security assurance.

. Customer data stored in our databases is encrypted using AES-256-GCM. Encryption keys are managed through a dedicated hardware security module (HSM) with automatic key rotation every 90 days. Database backups are encrypted with separate keys and stored in a different region from the primary data store.

. For customers handling particularly sensitive data — such as healthcare, legal, or financial information — we offer an end-to-end encrypted relay option. In this mode, prompts are encrypted on the client side before being sent to our API and are only decrypted inside the model inference environment. This means that even GlbTOKEN's infrastructure never has access to the plaintext content of your requests.

. For enterprise customers, SOC 2 reports are often required before procurement teams can approve a new vendor. Our certification means we can provide your compliance team with our SOC 2 report directly, dramatically shortening the vendor risk assessment process — from weeks to days.
. For general inquiries, our support team is available 24/7 through the in-app chat.
. SOC 2 Type II isn't a one-and-done certification. We undergo continuous monitoring and annual re-audits to maintain our certification. Security is never static, and neither is our commitment to it.

. This milestone represents a significant investment in the security, availability, and confidentiality of our platform — and it means that every customer, from individual developers to large enterprises, can trust GlbTOKEN with their AI workloads and sensitive data.

API Key Scoping.
API keys can be restricted by model, by maximum spend, by IP range, and by allowed endpoints. This lets you issue keys to contractors or CI/CD pipelines with confidence.
Access Control & Audit Logging

All administrative actions, API key changes, billing operations, and access attempts are recorded in tamper-evident audit logs. These logs are stored in write-once, read-many (WORM) storage and retained for a minimum of 12 months. Enterprise customers receive access to their audit log stream via secure webhook.

Our Commitment
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).

SOC 2 (Service Organization Control 2) is a rigorous auditing standard developed by the American Institute of CPAs (AICPA). Type II certification goes beyond a simple policy review — it requires demonstrating that security controls have been operating effectively over an extended period, typically six to twelve months. Our certification covers the five trust service criteria that matter most to our users:

Security certifications matter, but what matters more is the day-to-day practice of staying secure. GlbTOKEN runs a comprehensive vulnerability management program that includes:

Security is not a feature — it's a practice. SOC 2 Type II certification validates that we have the right practices in place, tested over time, and independently verified. But we don't stop there. Every team member at GlbTOKEN receives security awareness training on day one and annually thereafter. Every code change goes through mandatory peer review with automated security scanning. Every infrastructure change is reviewed through a change advisory board process.
Streamlined Vendor Reviews
Team accounts support Admin, Developer, Billing, and Read-Only roles with granular permissions for each.

The API gateway enforces strict tenant-aware access control on every request. Authentication tokens are scoped to a single customer ID, and the application layer verifies this binding before executing any operation.

— Information designated as confidential is protected as committed. All API traffic is encrypted with TLS 1.3 in transit. At rest, all data is encrypted using AES-256 with separate key management per customer data store. Database-level row-level security ensures that even within our infrastructure, one customer's data is never visible to another. Customer prompts and model outputs are never used for training or fine-tuning by any provider on our platform — this is enforced both by contract and by technical controls that prevent data egress.

— Personal information is collected, used, retained, disclosed, and disposed of in conformity with the commitments in our privacy policy. We comply with GDPR, CCPA, and other applicable privacy regulations. Data retention policies are automated: prompt data is purged within 30 days unless a customer explicitly opts into longer retention. We provide data deletion APIs and self-service account deletion through the dashboard.

— System processing is complete, valid, accurate, timely, and authorized. Every API request is validated, authenticated, authorized, and rate-limited before it reaches any model inference engine. Token accounting is verified through redundant counters with cross-validation — we audit every token deduction against our billing ledger to ensure you are never overcharged. Automated reconciliation runs every hour.

— The system is available for operation and use as committed. GlbTOKEN operates on a multi-region, multi-AZ (Availability Zone) architecture with automated failover. Our uptime SLA is 99.9% for the Developer API and 99.95% for Enterprise customers. During the SOC 2 audit period, we maintained 99.97% uptime across all services — exceeding our target by a wide margin. We run chaos engineering experiments monthly to validate our resilience against infrastructure failures.

— The system is protected against unauthorized access, both physical and logical. Our infrastructure runs on purpose-built cloud hardware with strict network segmentation, multi-factor authentication for all administrative access, and 24/7 intrusion detection. API keys are stored as salted bcrypt hashes and never logged or transmitted in plaintext. We maintain a formal security incident response plan that is tested quarterly through tabletop exercises and live drills.
— a detailed 20-page document covering our architecture, encryption, access controls, incident response, and compliance posture.
— a publicly available summary of our SOC 2 Type II report, expected in Q3 2026.

500 bonus tokens
API Key Management.
Accessibility improvements based on WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines

All existing users are automatically enrolled in the beta. There's nothing to install, no separate app to find, and no beta sign-up form to fill out. Just log in from your phone and the mobile interface appears automatically. Your balance, API keys, and history are all right where you left them.
Biometric authentication
Buy Tokens.
Cross-platform by design.

Dark mode is fully supported and automatically matches your system preference. The entire interface uses system fonts (San Francisco on iOS, Roboto on Android) for maximum readability at small sizes. Touch targets are at least 44x44 points, exceeding Apple's Human Interface Guidelines and Google's Material Design recommendations.

Depending on community interest, we may also explore a lightweight desktop Electron app or a dedicated Telegram mini-app for even faster access. But for now, we're focused on making the mobile web experience the best it can be.
Designed for Mobile First
For beta testers who report significant bugs or contribute valuable feature suggestions, we're offering
Full Dashboard.
Full internationalization with all languages currently supported on desktop

Generate, revoke, and manage API keys from your phone. Each key card shows usage statistics, the last used timestamp, and any spend limits you've set. You can copy the full key to your clipboard with a single tap — no more squinting at long alphanumeric strings and typing them manually.
Getting started with the mobile web app couldn't be simpler:
GlbTOKEN mobile web app
How to Get Started
Instant updates.
June 15, 2026 · 5 min read
Mobile App Beta: Manage Your Balance on the Go
Mobile App Beta: Manage Your Balance on the Go — GlbTOKEN Blog

Native apps often need to sync data between devices. With a web app, you just log in. Your dashboard, your balance, your API keys — they're all there, instantly, because the data lives on our servers, not on your device. Switch between your phone and laptop seamlessly.

Native apps require downloading, installing, waiting for updates, and managing storage. A progressive web app (PWA) loads instantly from your browser. Open Safari, Chrome, or any modern mobile browser, navigate to glbtoken.com, log in — and you have the full experience. For Android users, you can even "Add to Home Screen" for a native-style icon and full-screen experience.
Navigate to
No data sync headaches.
Notifications.
Offline mode
Open Safari, Chrome, Firefox, or any modern browser on your phone or tablet.

Our in-app support chat is fully functional on mobile. Whether you have a question about billing, need help with a model, or want to report an issue, the support button is always accessible from the bottom-right corner of the screen. The chat window is draggable and resizable even on mobile, and messages sync across devices so you can start a conversation on your phone and continue it on desktop.

Over the past several months, we've heard a clear message from our users: managing AI token balances, monitoring usage, and buying more tokens shouldn't require sitting at a desktop. Whether you're a developer checking API usage between meetings, a freelancer topping up from a coffee shop, or a team lead approving budget increases on the go — you need access to your GlbTOKEN account wherever you are. Our mobile web app delivers exactly that.
Performance optimization for low-end devices and slower networks
QR code payments

Receive real-time push notifications for low balance warnings, successful top-ups, API key usage alerts, and new model announcements. Since the app runs in your browser, notifications work even when the tab is closed (on supported browsers). Never run out of tokens unexpectedly again.
Shipping the features listed in our development roadmap above
Sign up or log in from your phone
Stabilizing the core experience based on real-world usage data
Support Chat.
That's it. You'll see the mobile-optimized dashboard automatically. For the best experience, Android users can tap "Add to Home Screen" from the browser menu to install the PWA.
The beta launch covers the core functionality, but we're actively working on several features that didn't make the initial cut:
The beta period will run for approximately 4-6 weeks, during which we'll be iterating rapidly based on user feedback. After that, we'll move to general availability with the following priorities:
The mobile web app includes nearly every feature from the desktop dashboard, carefully reorganized for smaller screens. Here's what's available in the beta:

The mobile web app isn't just the desktop site squeezed into a smaller viewport. We built it mobile-first from the ground up. Every interaction was reconsidered for touch: buttons are larger, forms use native input types (numeric keyboards for amounts, email keyboards for login fields), and navigation uses a bottom-sheet drawer that's thumb-friendly.

The top-up flow has been completely redesigned for mobile. Select an amount, choose your payment method (credit card, USDT, BTC, or Paystack for African users), and complete the purchase — all within the browser. We've integrated Apple Pay and Google Pay for one-tap purchases on supported devices, making it possible to top up your balance in under ten seconds.

This is a beta, and that means your feedback is critical. We want to know what works, what doesn't, and what's missing. Every piece of feedback — whether it's a broken layout on a specific device, a feature you'd love to see, or a performance issue — helps us make the mobile app better for everyone.

Track your token consumption by model, by time period, and by API key. The charts are interactive and touch-friendly — pinch to zoom, tap to see exact values, swipe between time ranges. You can see at a glance which models are consuming the most tokens and set up spending alerts directly from the mobile interface.
Usage Analytics.

View your current token balance, recent transactions, and usage history at a glance. The dashboard is optimized for mobile with larger tap targets, swipeable cards, and a streamlined layout that puts the most important information front and center. Your token balance is prominently displayed at the top, and recent API usage is visualized in a compact chart that works beautifully on small screens.
Voice commands
We Want Your Feedback

We believe that managing your AI token balance should be as easy as checking your bank balance — accessible anywhere, anytime, on any device. The mobile web app beta is a big step toward that vision, and we're excited to have you along for the ride.

We paid special attention to performance. The mobile app loads in under two seconds on 4G connections, with critical CSS inlined and JavaScript lazy-loaded. Images are served in next-gen formats (WebP) at appropriate resolutions for each device. The result is a snappy, native-feeling experience that doesn't drain your battery or eat up your data plan.
We're excited to announce the beta launch of the
What You Can Do in the Mobile App
What's Next After Beta
What's Still in Development

When we ship a new feature or fix a bug, it's live immediately. No waiting for app store review cycles that can take days. No prompting users to download version 2.4.3. The mobile web app is always up to date because it's just a web page.

Whether you use an iPhone, a Samsung Galaxy, a Pixel, a tablet, or even a foldable — it works. One codebase, every device. We didn't want to build an iOS app, then an Android app, then a tablet version, and still leave out Windows Phone or KaiOS users. The mobile web app works everywhere.
Why a Mobile Web App?
Widget support
You can share feedback directly through the in-app support chat (the 💬 button in the bottom-right corner). Alternatively, email us at
You might be asking: why a mobile web app instead of a native iOS/Android app? The answer comes down to three core principles we believe in at GlbTOKEN:
Zero installation.

blog.html: Mobile App Beta: Manage Your Balance on the Go
Our OpenAI-compatible API is now live. Generate a key and start building with any model in minutes. Full documentation in the Developer section.
Our mobile web app is now in beta — optimized for mobile browsers with a full dashboard, buy tokens, and support chat. No app store download needed.
Users in Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, and South Africa can now pay with local currencies via Paystack. Bank transfer, USSD, and mobile money supported.
We're excited to launch GlbTOKEN, the unified token for accessing 100+ AI models. No more juggling subscriptions — one balance, one API, unlimited AI.
We've added the latest flagship models from OpenAI and Anthropic. GPT-5.5 Pro and Claude Sonnet 5 are now available through the GlbTOKEN API.
We've completed SOC 2 Type II certification. All API traffic is now encrypted end-to-end, and user data is isolated at the database level. Enterprise customers can request a security whitepaper.
